# Earn Service Provider

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)

The Earn Service Provider backs Earn Kit with Circle Earn service APIs. It
handles vault discovery, position lookups, quote requests, signed execution
payloads, cross-chain bridge deposit prepare and submit requests, typed
response validation, and Earn-specific API error parsing.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @circle-fin/provider-earn-service
# or
yarn add @circle-fin/provider-earn-service
```

This provider is included by default in `@circle-fin/earn-kit`. Import it
directly when you need to customize provider construction or compose your own
Earn Kit context.

## Quick Start

```typescript
import {
  EarnChain,
  createEarnKitContext,
  getVaults,
} from '@circle-fin/earn-kit'
import { EarnServiceProvider } from '@circle-fin/provider-earn-service'

const provider = new EarnServiceProvider()
const context = createEarnKitContext({ providers: [provider] })

const result = await getVaults(context, {
  vaults: [
    {
      chain: EarnChain.Arc_Testnet,
      vaultAddress: '0x8eB67A509616cd6A7c1B3c8C21D48FF57df3d458',
    },
  ],
})

console.log(`Found ${result.vaults.length} vaults`)
```

## Configuration

`EarnServiceProvider` accepts default configuration in the constructor and
per-operation configuration on each request. Per-operation values take
precedence, while omitted values continue to use constructor defaults.

```typescript
const provider = new EarnServiceProvider({
  baseUrl: 'https://api.circle.com',
})

await provider.getVaults({
  vaults: [
    {
      chain: 'Arc_Testnet',
      vaultAddress: '0x8eB67A509616cd6A7c1B3c8C21D48FF57df3d458',
    },
  ],
  config: {
    kitKey: process.env.KIT_KEY,
  },
})
```

## Supported Chains

The initial provider release supports Arc Testnet earn flows. Same-chain
deposits use the existing approval and Earn action execution path.

Cross-chain deposits are available when the request has a supported EVM source
chain, a supported Earn destination chain, matching testnet or mainnet
environments, source token configuration, CCTP routing, and bridge adapter
configuration. The provider validates those route requirements before calling
the bridge prepare endpoint.
